executiveSummary:
**Medicaid Program Performance Analysis - January 2026**

National Medicaid enrollment has stabilized at 75.3 million beneficiaries as of January 2026, though most states continue experiencing enrollment declines following the end of pandemic-era continuous coverage protections. Among major states, enrollment decreases ranged from 1.8% in New York to 7.6% in Michigan year-over-year, with only North Carolina posting modest growth at 0.5%. Texas, maintaining its position as the third-largest Medicaid state with 4.0 million enrollees, experienced a moderate 3.1% decline—comparable to Pennsylvania's decrease and better than the 6.2% drops seen in California and Illinois. National spending reached $215.9 billion in fiscal year 2024, with per-enrollee costs averaging $2,869. Texas demonstrated relatively efficient spending at $2,988 per enrollee, positioned between high-cost states like Pennsylvania ($4,377) and Massachusetts ($4,201) and lower-cost programs in Florida ($2,031) and North Carolina ($2,616).

Quality performance presents a mixed national picture, with the average state scoring 65.6 out of 100 points in 2024. While ten states achieved high performance ratings above 75 points, led by Massachusetts (81) and Minnesota (80), fifteen states scored below 60 points. Texas ranked 30th nationally with a score of 62, placing it in the moderate performance tier but trailing quality leaders by significant margins. However, Texas demonstrates strong managed care infrastructure with 93.3% penetration—well above the national average of 83.2%—suggesting robust delivery system organization. Recent federal regulatory activity includes new provider enrollment moratoria and updated state directed payment rules, while continued HHS OIG oversight emphasizes the importance of maintaining compliance across all program areas as states navigate post-pandemic program adjustments.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Analysis

Texas operates the nation's third-largest Medicaid program with 4.03 million enrollees, serving a substantial population despite its non-expansion status. The state's enrollment declined 3.1% year-over-year, which aligns closely with the national trend of post-pandemic unwinding but outperforms several peer states including Florida (-4.0%) and Ohio (-4.4%). However, Texas lags behind North Carolina (+0.5%), the only large state showing enrollment growth. At $2,988 per enrollee, Texas maintains relatively efficient spending compared to the national average of $2,869, though this figure sits in the middle range among large states—significantly lower than high-cost states like Pennsylvania ($4,377) and New York ($3,431), but higher than Florida's notably lean $2,031 per enrollee. The state's robust managed care penetration of 93.3% substantially exceeds the national average of 83.2%, indicating strong administrative coordination through private plan partnerships.

**Quality performance remains Texas's most significant challenge, with a score of 62/100 ranking 30th nationally and falling below the national average of 65.6.** This places Texas in the bottom half among all states and significantly behind quality leaders like Massachusetts (81) and Minnesota (80). Compared to peer southern states, Texas performs moderately—better than the region's lowest performers like Mississippi (48) and Alabama (52), but trailing potential benchmarks. The combination of efficient per-enrollee spending and high managed care penetration suggests administrative competency, but the quality gap represents a critical area requiring focused attention. Key areas to monitor include whether ongoing federal regulatory changes around state-directed payments and provider enrollment moratoria will impact Texas's managed care operations, and whether the state can leverage its strong administrative infrastructure to drive quality improvements without substantially increasing per-enrollee costs.

alerts.title: Pennsylvania Per-Enrollee Spending 53% Above National Average
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,377 is significantly higher than the national average of $2,869, indicating potential cost management issues.

alerts.title: Michigan Enrollment Declined 7.6% Year-over-Year
alerts.description: Michigan experienced enrollment decline well above the 10% threshold for concern, suggesting potential access or eligibility issues.

alerts.title: Five States Show Critically Low Quality Performance
alerts.description: Arkansas, South Carolina, Alabama, Oklahoma, and Mississippi all scored below 55 on quality measures, with Mississippi at only 48.

alerts.title: Florida Shows High Efficiency with Low Per-Enrollee Costs
alerts.description: Florida achieves significantly lower per-enrollee spending ($2,031) compared to the national average ($2,869), potentially indicating efficient program management.

alerts.title: Massachusetts Leads Quality Despite High Per-Enrollee Spending
alerts.description: Massachusetts has the highest quality score (81) but also high per-enrollee spending ($4,201), suggesting a potential quality-cost trade-off model.

executiveInsights.title: Texas quality score below national avg - quality improvement urgent
executiveInsights.summary: Texas ranks #30 of 50 states with a quality score of 62/100, below the national average of 65.6. This places Texas in the moderate tier, significantly behind top performers like MA (81) and MN (80).
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Poor quality performance creates regulatory risk and potential penalties for HHSC. Accenture should prioritize quality improvement initiatives to protect contract performance.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag quality improvement as immediate priority to HHSC leadership

executiveInsights.title: Texas per-enrollee costs above national average by $119
executiveInsights.summary: Texas spends $2,988 per enrollee compared to the national average of $2,869, representing a 4.1% premium. With 4M+ enrollees, this translates to ~$480M in excess spending annually.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Higher per-enrollee costs indicate potential inefficiencies that HHSC leadership will want to address. This creates opportunity for Accenture to propose cost optimization initiatives.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review cost optimization opportunities with HHSC finance team

executiveInsights.title: Texas enrollment declining faster than neighboring states
executiveInsights.summary: Texas enrollment dropped 3.1% YoY while NC grew 0.5%. Texas decline matches PA but exceeds the more moderate declines in similar large states.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Enrollment decline may signal eligibility redetermination issues or operational challenges that require immediate attention to prevent further losses.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review enrollment retention strategies with HHSC operations team

executiveInsights.title: New federal regulations on state directed payments effective
executiveInsights.summary: Recent Federal Register entry shows new CMS rules on Medicaid managed care state directed payments are being implemented. Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration, well above national average.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: High managed care penetration makes Texas highly exposed to these regulatory changes. Compliance gaps could create significant financial and operational risks.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag new state directed payment regulations to HHSC compliance team

executiveInsights.title: Texas managed care penetration significantly above national average
executiveInsights.summary: Texas managed care penetration is 93.3% compared to national average of 83.2%. This represents 3.86M managed care enrollees out of 4.03M total.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: High managed care penetration creates both operational efficiency opportunities and regulatory compliance complexities that Accenture should leverage in service delivery.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review managed care optimization opportunities with HHSC

riskOpportunity.label: EVV System Modernization
riskOpportunity.description: Texas requires updated Electronic Visit Verification systems for home and community-based services compliance and fraud prevention.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Position Accenture's proven EVV platform capabilities in upcoming HHSC technology procurements.

riskOpportunity.label: Managed Care Recompete
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's 93.3% managed care penetration creates high-stakes contract renewals worth billions in annual expenditures.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop comprehensive managed care operations and analytics proposals for HHSC's next procurement cycle.

riskOpportunity.label: Quality Improvement Initiative
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks 30th nationally with a 62/100 quality score, creating pressure for performance improvement programs.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose quality analytics and improvement consulting services to help Texas reach top-tier performance levels.

riskOpportunity.label: Federal Compliance Risk
riskOpportunity.description: HHS OIG active oversight and new CMS directed payment regulations create compliance complexity for Texas operations.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish dedicated regulatory compliance monitoring and advisory services for HHSC leadership.

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment Decline Management
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's 3.1% YoY enrollment decline requires operational adjustments and cost optimization strategies.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop enrollment stabilization and operational efficiency consulting offerings for declining membership scenarios.

riskOpportunity.label: Data Analytics Platform
riskOpportunity.description: Texas's $12B program expenditures and complex managed care structure demand advanced analytics for cost containment and outcomes.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose integrated data platform solutions combining claims, quality, and operational analytics for HHSC decision-making.

riskOpportunity.label: Provider Network Optimization
riskOpportunity.description: High managed care penetration and per-enrollee costs below national average suggest network adequacy and payment optimization needs.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Design provider network adequacy and payment reform consulting services to optimize access and costs.

riskOpportunity.label: Competitive Displacement Risk
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple large-scale Texas procurements create opportunities for competitors to displace Accenture's current market position.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Strengthen existing HHSC relationships through proactive value delivery and strategic partnership initiatives.
